topic: fractions and decimals on number line
latex: On the number line, let $A=12.3$ and let $B=\dfrac{25}{2}$. What is the value of $C$, as a decimal to the nearest hundredth, if $C$ is three-quarters of the way from $A$ to $B$?
notes: }  \textbf{Answer:} In decimal form, $B=12.5$, so the length of $\overline{AB}=12.5-12.3=0.2$. Three-quarters of $0.2$ is $\dfrac{3}{4}(0.2)=0.15$. Since $C$ is three-quarters of the way from $A$ to $B$, we start at $A$ and add $0.15$, to find that $C=12.3+0.15=\boxed{12.45}$. }
